Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 108 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 23 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 73 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 246 upvotes, 37 comments
  5. Nmap in the movies (2008) 136 upvotes, 18 comments
  6. Generalizing Knuth's Pseudocode Architecture From Algorithms to Knowledge 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Marketing for Founders 54 upvotes, 9 comments
  8. GIMP 3.2 released 155 upvotes, 37 comments
  9. Fedora 44 on the Raspberry Pi 5 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 56 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. Python: The Optimization Ladder 234 upvotes, 83 comments
  12. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1090 upvotes, 467 comments
  13. Sunsetting Jazzband 110 upvotes, 37 comments
  14. The Washington Post Is Using Reader Data to Set Subscription Prices 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. CSMWrap: Legacy BIOS booting on UEFI-only systems via SeaBIOS 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  16. A Recursive Algorithm to Render Signed Distance Fields 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  17. Offloading FFmpeg with Cloudflare 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. Show HN: Ichinichi – One note per day, E2E encrypted, local-first 37 upvotes, 16 comments
  19. Claude March 2026 usage promotion 77 upvotes, 42 comments
  20. Hostile Volume – A game about adjusting volume with intentionally bad UI 59 upvotes, 36 comments
  21. It's time to move your docs in the repo 74 upvotes, 49 comments
  22. An ode to bzip 70 upvotes, 46 comments
  23. 2026 tech layoffs reach 45,000 in March 126 upvotes, 98 comments
  24. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 221 upvotes, 186 comments
  25. MCP is dead; long live MCP 72 upvotes, 57 comments
  26. XML is a cheap DSL 216 upvotes, 226 comments
  27. Library of Short Stories 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. Life as an OnlyFans 'chatter' 111 upvotes, 136 comments
  29. Starlink militarization and its impact on global strategic stability 95 upvotes, 118 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 15 March 2026. Source here.
